user_query:
Please read input/article.md (the source article) and input/brand_guidelines.md (brand voice). Then deliver all of the following, writing every file under output/:

1) LinkedIn posts (professional tone): Generate 5 LinkedIn-ready post variations summarizing the article. Each post must:
- Be under 1200 characters
- Include 2–4 relevant hashtags
- End with the exact brand attribution: "— Acme Growth Lab"
- Reflect the key ideas from the article (e.g., references such as "90-day", "developer-first", "activation", "retention", or "ship weekly")
Format: In output/linkedin_posts.md, separate posts with a line containing only three dashes (---) and label each section with a line "Post 1", "Post 2", … "Post 5".

2) Twitter thread (casual tone): Convert the article into a 6-tweet thread and save to output/twitter_thread.md. Each tweet must:
- Start with its number marker exactly as "1/6", "2/6", … "6/6" on the same line as the tweet text
- Be 280 characters or fewer (including the number marker)
- Include at least one relevant hashtag per tweet
- End the last tweet (6/6) with the exact brand attribution: "— Acme Growth Lab"
- Summarize the article’s core insights in a practical, friendly voice

3) Viral hooks (platform-agnostic): Create 10 distinct, one-line opening hooks suitable for social posts that would promote this article. No hashtags and no brand attribution in the hooks. Number them exactly as "1) ..." through "10) ..." (each on its own line). Save to output/viral_hooks.md.

4) Metadata: Create output/metadata.json containing summary stats:
{
  "linkedin_posts": {"count": 5, "avg_chars": <average character count across the 5 LinkedIn posts>},
  "twitter_thread": {"count": 6, "avg_chars": <average character count across the 6 tweets>}
}
Ensure the averages reflect the actual output you wrote.

Please ensure all content aligns with the brand voice in input/brand_guidelines.md and that the LinkedIn posts are professional while the thread reads more casual. Do not include external links.
